title: Table Filter and Search Function
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to filter and search the Data Dictionary table by field attributes, so that I can quickly find and focus on specific fields without scrolling through the entire list.
description:
The data table in the Migration and Dictionary views needs filtering and search capabilities to help users locate specific fields.

Requirements:
1. Implement column-level filtering using DataTables FixedHeader column filtering extension.
2. Provide a toggle icon to show/hide the column filters (hidden by default, slide-in via jQuery animation).
3. For dropdown-style columns (e.g., boolean fields), use dropdown filters with 'All / Yes / No' options instead of free-text input.

After initial implementation, the approach was refined: a custom search box was added to the toolbar row alongside filters, and the built-in DataTables search was hidden. The 'Filter by field name' input was temporarily hidden in favor of the unified search box.
acceptance_criteria: A custom search box is positioned in the toolbar row
Search box filters the entire table across all visible columns
Built-in DataTables search input is hidden in favor of the custom search box
Column filters use dropdown selectors for boolean/enumerated columns
Filtering and search work in both Migration and Dictionary views
ux_changes: Adds a custom search input in the toolbar row that filters the data table using the DataTables API. Column-level filters were initially implemented but the field name filter was temporarily hidden in favor of the unified search. Boolean columns use dropdown filter controls.
implementation_notes: Uses the DataTables API `search()` method triggered by a custom input field's keyup event. The built-in search input is hidden via CSS. Column filtering uses the FixedHeader extension. V1 shipped with the unified search approach — per-column filtering may be revisited based on user feedback.
comment_summary: Developer asked whether per-column filters were needed in addition to existing top-level filters. Product owner decided current implementation was sufficient for V1. A follow-up enhancement moved the search box inline with the toolbar and temporarily hid the field name filter for a cleaner UX.
